findings.title: Dependency 'ghost-payments-sdk' added but absent from lockfile (possible hallucinated package)
findings.evidence: raw diff adds "ghost-payments-sdk": "^1.0.0" to dependencies. Report flags it as not present in the lockfile. The name is suspicious for a payment SDK on a checkout-service and could be a hallucinated/typosquatted package, which would cause arbitrary install-time code or a dependency-confusion vector. Existence and provenance must be confirmed before trust.

findings.title: New postinstall script executes scripts/bootstrap.js during install
findings.evidence: raw diff adds "postinstall": "node scripts/bootstrap.js". postinstall runs automatically on every npm install with no opt-in. The referenced scripts/bootstrap.js is not included in this diff, so its behavior is unreviewable; combined with the unverified new dependency this warrants investigation before merge.

notes: Only one file (package.json) changed; the Diff Drift report and raw diff are consistent with each other (2 additions, 2 flags).

No lockfile (package-lock.json / yarn.lock / pnpm-lock.yaml) appears in the diff, so the 'not in lockfile' claim cannot be independently verified from the packet — but the absence of any lockfile update for a newly added dependency is itself a reason to investigate.
scripts/bootstrap.js is referenced by the new postinstall script but its contents are not part of this diff, so the actual install-time behavior could not be reviewed.

Decision is 'investigate' rather than 'block' because nothing in the reviewable diff is provably malicious; the risk is unverified provenance (package existence, lockfile, bootstrap script contents) that a reviewer should confirm before trusting the change.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
